steps.title: Find the antecedent
steps.content: Strip the dash-clause: "When a given industry—water and electricity are two well-known examples—carries high infrastructural start-up costs..., ___ of just one or two suppliers per municipality." The subject of the main clause refers back to industry — singular.

steps.title: Apply the rule
steps.content:
Singular antecedent → singular pronoun and singular verb: it often consists.

A and C use plural pronouns (they, these) — wrong number. B uses this, which doesn't refer cleanly to "industry" the way it does, and isn't the SAT-preferred choice here.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
